title: kobresia royleana var. paniculata
intro: Kobresia royleana var. paniculata, also known as Indian club sedge, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. Traditionally, it has been used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health issues, with its medicinal properties attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory problems, and promoting digestion. In herbal preparations, Kobresia royleana var. paniculata is often used in decoctions, infusions, or powders, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
